title: Inconsistent Trie Insertion Causing Duplicate Nodes

statement.markdown: Given a trie and a set of strings, insert each string into the trie. However, due to a bug, the insertion logic incorrectly creates duplicate nodes for the same string, causing unintended behavior. Fix the bug to implement correct trie insertion.
statement.inputFormat: The first line contains the number of strings, n. The following n lines contain a string to insert into the trie.
statement.notes: Time complexity: O(n * m), where n is the number of strings and m is the maximum length of a string.

examples.explanation: After inserting the strings, the trie should store the following paths: /apple /apply /banana

hints.text: Review the trie insertion logic to identify the cause of duplicate nodes.

editorial.title: Editorial
editorial.intuition: Correctly implement trie insertion by handling duplicate nodes.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute-force approach would involve iterating over each string to insert and checking for duplicate nodes after each insertion.
editorial.optimalSolution: To avoid duplicate nodes, keep track of existing nodes and only create a new node if a node with the same key does not already exist.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The corrected trie insertion logic ensures that each string is inserted only once, and no duplicate nodes are created.
